import io
import string
import list

fn is_ws(_ b: Int) -> Bool
  b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
end

fn is_digit(_ b: Int) -> Bool
  b >= 48 and b <= 57
end

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut outer = true
  while outer
    mut scanning = true
    while scanning and i < n
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(b)
        if is_ws(b)
          i = i + 1
        else
          scanning = false
        end
      when None
        scanning = false
      end
    end
    if i >= n
      outer = false
    else
      mut sign = 1
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(b)
        if b == 45
          sign = -1
          i = i + 1
        else if b == 43
          i = i + 1
        else
          sign = 1
        end
      when None
        sign = 1
      end
      mut val = 0
      mut parsing = true
      while parsing and i < n
        match string.byte_at(s, i)
        when Some(b)
          if is_digit(b)
            val = val * 10 + (b - 48)
            i = i + 1
          else
            parsing = false
          end
        when None
          parsing = false
        end
      end
      result = list.push(result, val * sign)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn emit(_ a: Int, _ b: Int, _ s: Int) -> Unit
  mut out = ""
  mut v = a
  mut first = true
  while v < b
    if first
      out = "#{v}"
      first = false
    else
      out = "#{out} #{v}"
    end
    v = v + s
  end
  println(out)
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let input = io.read_to_end()
  let nums = parse_ints(input)
  match nums.get(0)
  when Some(a)
    match nums.get(1)
    when Some(b)
      match nums.get(2)
      when Some(s)
        emit(a, b, s)
      when None
        println("")
      end
    when None
      println("")
    end
  when None
    println("")
  end
end
